title: Eliea articulata
uses: Eliea articulata is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its extracts have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Eliea articulata is also used to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses.
taxonomy: Eliea articulata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is a member of the genus Eliea, which is a small group of plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Boraginaceae is a large and diverse group that includes over 2,000 species of flowering plants. Boraginaceae plants are known for their distinctive flowers and seeds, and are often found in a variety of habitats around the world. The classification of Eliea articulata reflects its unique characteristics and its place within the broader family of Boraginaceae plants.

side_effects: Eliea articulata, also known as Elaeis guineensis or oil palm, is commonly used for its edible oil. The oil also contains high levels of saturated fats, which can contribute to cardiovascular issues when ingested in large amounts.
